steps.title: Identify the central claim
steps.content: Despite the sonnet's reputation for strict formal requirements, the text argues that the form actually accommodates wide-ranging variety. Phillips's point: when a form's conventions are familiar, the urge to subvert them grows.

steps.title: Match the main idea
steps.content:
Choice B captures both halves: the sonnet encourages surprising variety, even though its formal characteristics make this unlikely.

  • A focuses on two poems' celebrated status (off-topic)
  • C is about popularity (off-topic)
  • D claims a long stretch of no innovation (unsupported)
.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
